title: 10 best herbal creams for oral thrush

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial and antibacterial properties: These properties help to combat the fungal infection that causes oral thrush, reducing the risk of spreading the infection.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold creams can help to reduce inflammation and soothe the oral tissues, making it easier to manage symptoms such as mouth pain and difficulty swallowing.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold creams can help to protect the oral tissues from oxidative stress and damage caused by the infection, promoting faster healing and recovery.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with oral thrush because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, work to reduce inflammation and kill fungal pathogens that cause oral thrush. By soothing the mucous membranes and promoting a healthy environment, Calendula officinalis creams can help to alleviate symptoms of oral thrush, including pain, redness, and white patches. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective solution for treating oral thrush.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce inflammation and combat the fungal growth that causes oral thrush.
Phenolic acids: These compounds exhibit antifungal and antioxidant properties, which can help prevent the spread of oral thrush and promote healing of the affected area.
Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and combat the fungal infection that causes oral thrush.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in triterpenoids and flavonoids, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help combat oral thrush.
Leaves: They contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and soothe the oral cavity, making them suitable for treating oral thrush.
Seeds: They have antimicrobial properties, which help combat fungal infections like oral thrush, and also contain antioxidants that promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers in their peak bloom stage when they are fully open and dry.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm place with good air circulation for 2-3 weeks.
Infuse 30 grams of dried flowers in 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and mix it with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10-15 minutes until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Lemon balm's antifungal properties help to inhibit the growth of Candida, the fungus responsible for oral thrush.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the inflammation and discomfort caused by oral thrush.
Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm's antimicrobial properties help to reduce the presence of bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to oral thrush.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with oral thrush because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties. The active compounds in Melissa officinalis, such as rosmarinic acid and citral, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. The cream's soothing and anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce discomfort and pain associated with oral thrush, allowing the affected area to heal quickly and promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has antimicrobial properties, helping to combat the fungal growth that causes oral thrush, thereby reducing its severity and duration.
Limonene: As a terpene, limonene exhibits antifungal activity, which aids in controlling the Candida albicans fungus responsible for oral thrush.

Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound possesses anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce the discomfort and inflammation associated with oral thrush, while its antimicrobial properties aid in controlling fungal growth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Melissa officinalis in creams for oral thrush due to their antifungal and antimicrobial properties.
Stems: Stems of Melissa officinalis are used in creams for oral thrush due to their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe the affected area.
Flowers: Flowers of Melissa officinalis are used in creams for oral thrush due to their antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, which help prevent infection and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis flowers and leaves when they are in full bloom and the plant is in its prime state.
Dry the harvested melissa officinalis flowers and leaves in a warm and well-ventilated area for 1-2 weeks.
Infuse the dried melissa officinalis flowers and leaves in a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:5.
Strain the infused oil and add a preservative such as vitamin e oil to prevent spoilage and extend shelf life.
Mix the strained oil with a thickening agent such as beeswax and a moisturizing ingredient like shea butter to create a smooth and creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation and soothe the irritated mucous membranes in the mouth, making it easier to manage oral thrush symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with oral thrush because of its soothing and antimicrobial properties. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and discomfort in the mouth, while its antimicrobial properties combat the fungal infection that causes oral thrush. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects can help alleviate the anxiety and stress that can exacerbate the condition. remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid present in Matricaria chamomilla, apigenin has been shown to exhibit antifungal properties, which can help combat the fungal growth causing oral thrush.
Luteolin: A flavonoid with antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, luteolin in Matricaria chamomilla creams may help soothe and heal the oral mucosa affected by oral thrush.

α-Bisabolol: A sesquiterpene alcohol found in Matricaria chamomilla, α-bisabolol has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in reducing inflammation and fighting off the fungal infection causing oral thrush.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help soothe and calm the oral mucosa affected by thrush.
Leaves: They have antimicrobial properties that help combat fungal infections such as oral thrush.
Seeds: They contain chamazulene, an anti-inflammatory compound that can help reduce discomfort and pain associated with oral thrush.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers, ensure they are clean and free of debris.
Infuse 2 cups of water in a saucepan with 1 cup of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers for 30 minutes.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container.
Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Combine the infused mixture with 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil and 2 tablespoons of the beeswax mixture.

remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe and calm the oral tissues affected by thrush, reducing redness and discomfort.
Antifungal activity: Dandelion's natural antifungal properties help inhibit the growth of the Candida fungus that causes oral thrush, allowing the body to fight off the infection.
Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidant properties help protect the oral tissues from damage caused by oxidative stress, promoting a healthy healing environment.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with oral thrush because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxeryl acetate, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the oral mucosa, reducing discomfort and promoting a healthy environment for the growth of beneficial microorganisms. This natural approach can aid in the management and treatment of oral thrush.
remedy_constituents: Apolyporone: A terpenoid saponin found in Taraxacum officinale, which may help inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush.
Taraxasterol: A triterpenoid saponin with antifungal properties, which may help reduce the severity of oral thrush by inhibiting the spread of Candida albicans.
Taraxasterol acetate: A derivative of taraxasterol with antifungal activity, which may help prevent the formation of biofilms by Candida albicans, thereby reducing the risk of oral thrush.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which help combat the fungal infection that causes oral thrush.
Roots: Utilized for their antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which aid in preventing the growth of Candida, the fungus responsible for oral thrush.
Seeds: Employed for their potential to exhibit antifungal and anti-inflammatory effects, which contribute to the healing process of oral thrush.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves, ideally in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Infuse the taraxacum officinale in 2 cups of boiling water for 10-15 minutes to create a tea.
Strain the tea and combine it with 1 cup of beeswax, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of shea butter.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160°f for 20-25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a smooth consistency.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken completely before transferring it to a clean, sterilized container for storage.

remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Kansas coneflower creams contain compounds such as triterpenoids and sesquiterpenes that have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal organisms, including Candida albicans, which causes oral thrush.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The creams also contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation and soothe the oral mucosa, making it an effective treatment for oral thrush symptoms such as pain and discomfort.
Immune system support: Kansas coneflower creams may also contain compounds that stimulate the immune system, helping to boost its natural defenses against fungal infections, including oral thrush.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, creams helps with oral thrush because of its potent antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as alkylamides and phenolic acids, work to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus that causes oral thrush. Additionally, Echinacea's soothing properties help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the mouth, promoting a healthy environment for the restoration of the natural oral flora. remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat the fungal infections that cause oral thrush by inhibiting the growth of Candida albicans.

Alkylamides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the inflammation and discomfort associated with oral thrush, making it easier for the body to fight off the infection.

remedy_parts: Roots: Echinacea angustifolia roots are commonly used due to their high concentration of compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help combat oral thrush.
Stems: Echinacea angustifolia stems contain essential oils and other bioactive compounds that exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, making them a suitable choice for treating oral thrush.

Barks: Echinacea angustifolia barks are used in creams due to their rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities that can aid in oral thrush treatment.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 30g of dried echinacea angustifolia flowers in 500ml of cold-pressed oil in a double boiler for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids.
Mix 100g of beeswax with 50g of shea butter in a double boiler until melted.
Combine the infused oil with the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture in a 3:1 ratio.
Pour the cream mixture into small containers and allow it to cool and solidify completely.

remedy_properties: Menthol content: Menthol has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help combat the fungal infection and soothe irritated tissues.
Tannins: Tannins in wintergreen have astringent properties that can help reduce fungal growth, kill Candida yeast, and prevent further infections.
Methyl salicylate: Methyl salicylate, a key component of wintergreen oil, has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling, fight off infection, and promote healing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gaultheria procumbens, also known as wintergreen, creams helps with oral thrush because of its antifungal properties. The cream's active ingredient, methyl salicylate, has been shown to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for causing oral thrush. remedy_constituents: Gentiopicroside: This iridoid glycoside has antifungal properties, which can help combat the fungal infections that cause oral thrush by inhibiting the growth of the Candida albicans fungus.
Tannins: These polyphenolic compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent the spread of the fungal infection in the mouth.

Terpenes: Specifically, the sesquiterpene fraction in Gaultheria procumbens has been shown to exhibit antifungal activity, which can help combat oral thrush by inhibiting the growth of the Candida albicans fungus.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their antifungal and antibacterial properties, which can help combat the fungal infection causing oral thrush.
Roots: The roots are used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in soothing and healing the affected areas in the mouth.
Barks: The barks of Gaultheria procumbens are used for their astringent properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems on a sunny day in late summer.
Dry the harvested gaultheria procumbens in a dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for 8 hours.
Create a decoction by steeping 50 grams of dried gaultheria procumbens in 500 milliliters of water for 20 minutes.
Mix 25 milliliters of the decoction with 25 grams of beeswax and 25 grams of coconut oil to create a base.
Add 5 milliliters of vitamin e oil to the base and whip it until it becomes creamy and smooth.

remedy_properties: Antifungal activity: Selfheal creams contain compounds that inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus that causes oral thrush, reducing its spread and severity.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Selfheal's flavonoids and phenolic acids help reduce inflammation and alleviate the discomfort, pain, and swelling associated with oral thrush.
Wound-healing and tissue repair: Selfheal's allantoin and mucilage content promote wound healing, tissue repair, and the regeneration of oral mucosa, which is essential for resolving oral thrush.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Prunella vulgaris, also known as selfheal, creams helps with oral thrush because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains compounds like ursolic acid and rosmarinic acid that have been shown to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. Roots: The roots of Prunella vulgaris are known for their antiseptic and antifungal properties, which make them suitable for treating oral thrush.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh prunella vulgaris leaves and flowers when they are in full bloom.
Dry the harvested prunella vulgaris mixture in a single layer at 50°c for 2 hours.
Combine 20g of dried prunella vulgaris with 100ml of distilled water and heat the mixture for 10 minutes.
Mix 30g of beeswax with 30g of coconut oil and 20ml of the prunella vulgaris infusion at 60°c for 5 minutes.
Fill a clean container with the herbal cream mixture and let it cool to room temperature before use.

remedy_constituents: Quercetin: This flavonoid acts as an antifungal agent, inhibiting the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush.

Stems: Stems are used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing the severity of oral thrush symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 bunches of fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems for a batch of herbal cream.
Dry the harvested urtica dioica in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Grind 30 grams of dried urtica dioica into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with 100 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture until it's fully melted and cooled slightly before pouring into jars for storage.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow creams reduce inflammation and swelling in the mouth, which can be a symptom of oral thrush.
Antimicrobial properties: Yarrow creams have been shown to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus that causes oral thrush.

remedy_recipe: Harvest the dried achillea millefolium flowers and leaves in quantities of 20 grams.
Steep the plant material in a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 hours.
Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove solids.
Add a preservative such as vitamin e oil to the oil mixture in a dosage of 5 milliliters.
Whip the cream with a hand mixer until it reaches the desired consistency in about 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Wound healing and anti-inflammatory properties: Comfrey cream's mucilages and flavonoids help soothe and calm the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing in the mouth.

Antimicrobial properties: Comfrey's tannins and other compounds have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which help to combat the Candida fungus that causes oral thrush, reducing its growth and severity.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried symphytum officinale root and 1 part of dried symphytum officinale leaves.
Infuse 1 part of dried symphytum officinale root in 2 parts of cold-pressed oil for 2 weeks.
Combine the infused oil with 1 part of beeswax and heat the mixture in a double boiler at 180-190 degrees fahrenheit.
Add 0.5 parts of distilled water to the mixture and stir until the beeswax is fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into a glass container and let it cool and solidify for 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with oral thrush because of its antifungal properties, which combat the overgrowth of Candida albicans that causes the infection. The active compounds in fennel, such as anethole and fenchone, inhibit the growth of Candida and soothe irritated mucous membranes. As a natural remedy, herbal fennel creams can be applied topically to the affected areas, promoting healing and reducing discomfort associated with oral thrush. Regular use can provide relief and help prevent future occurrences.

Seeds: Fennel seeds have antifungal properties that help to combat Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush, and promote healing.
Barks: Fennel barks contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds that exhibit antimicrobial activity, making them effective against oral thrush.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 grams of fresh foeniculum vulgare seeds or 20-25 grams of dried seeds.
Chop the foeniculum vulgare seeds into small pieces using a mortar and pestle or a food processor.
Mix the chopped seeds with 20 grams of beeswax, 10 grams of coconut oil, and 10 grams of shea butter.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160-180°f for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify completely before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood creams helps with oral thrush because of its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The herb wormwood contains thujone, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. When applied topically as a cream, wormwood's active compounds can directly target and reduce Candida overgrowth in the mouth, alleviating symptoms such as white patches and soreness. remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Wormwood's essential oils have been shown to exhibit potent antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help combat the fungal infections that cause oral thrush.
Inflammation reduction: Wormwood's flavonoids and terpenoids have been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with oral thrush.
Antioxidant activity: Wormwood's antioxidants may help reduce oxidative stress and promote healing in the mouth, which can aid in the treatment of oral thrush.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers and dry them immediately to preserve their potency for 2-3 hours.
Weigh 20-30 grams of dried artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers and mix with 10 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil for 5 minutes.
Heat the mixture of artemisia absinthium, beeswax, and coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 10-15 minutes.
Stir the mixture every 5 minutes to ensure even melting and incorporation of ingredients for 10-15 minutes.
Pour the hot mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30-40 minutes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain artemisinin, a compound with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: Rich in flavonoids and essential oils with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: Contains salicylic acid, a compound with antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with oral thrush because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to combat the fungal overgrowth that causes the condition. The tea tree oil and eucalyptus oil in the cream have been shown to be effective against Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. The cream's soothing and moisturizing properties also help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the affected area. Regular use of the cream can help to clear up symptoms and prevent recurrence.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Help kill fungi and bacteria that cause oral thrush, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Reduce swelling, pain, and discomfort associated with oral thrush, making it easier to manage symptoms.
Antifungal properties: Specifically target the fungus Candida albicans, which causes oral thrush, inhibiting its growth and spread.
remedy_parts: Barks: Anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties make the bark effective against oral infections and inflammation associated with thrush.
Rhyzomes: Rich in antimicrobial compounds that help combat fungal and bacterial infections in the mouth, reducing symptoms of thrush.
Leaves: Contain salicylic acid, which helps reduce inflammation and kills fungal spores responsible for oral thrush.
Stems: May be used to prepare extracts with antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can help soothe and heal oral mucosa affected by thrush.
Buds: May contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of oral thrush.
remedy_recipe: Extract eucalyptus globulus essential oil by steam distillation and measure 10ml of the obtained oil.
Prepare an infusion by steeping 2 tablespoons of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in 8oz of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the infusion and combine it with the extracted eucalyptus globulus essential oil, 1 teaspoon of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture is smooth and creamy, approximately 10 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from the heat, let it cool slightly, and pour the cream into a sterilized container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with oral thrush because of their antifungal properties. The cream can soothe and heal the mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing discomfort and inflammation. Elderberry, a key ingredient in many creams, is a natural antiviral and antibacterial agent that can help combat the fungal infection. Additionally, the cream can provide a physical barrier against fungal spores, reducing the risk of re-infection.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Herbal elder creams contain antimicrobial compounds that help to kill the fungal cells that cause oral thrush, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in elder creams help to reduce the redness and swelling associated with oral thrush, making it more comfortable for patients to swallow and eat.

Antiviral properties: Some herbal elder creams contain antiviral compounds that can help to fight off viral infections that may be contributing to the development of oral thrush, such as candidal infections.
remedy_recipe: Combine 20 grams of dried sambucus nigra flowers with 100 ml of cold-pressed coconut oil in a glass jar.
Stir the mixture well and let it infuse in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into another glass jar, discarding the solids.
Mix 5 grams of beeswax and 2 grams of shea butter with 100 ml of the infused oil in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture until the wax and butter are fully incorporated and the cream has reached a smooth consistency.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have antimicrobial properties, helping to combat fungal infections like oral thrush.
Barks: Contain phenolic compounds that exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, potentially aiding in the treatment of oral thrush.
Seeds: Possess saponins and other compounds with antimicrobial properties, which could contribute to the cream's effectiveness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops creams helps with oral thrush because of their antifungal properties, which target the underlying cause of the infection. The antifungal compounds in hops, such as lupulin, work to eliminate the overgrowth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. By reducing inflammation and soothing the affected areas, hops creams can also provide relief from discomfort and pain associated with oral thrush. This natural remedy can be a safe and effective treatment option.
remedy_properties: Antifungal and antibacterial properties: Herbal hops creams have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria that cause oral thrush, helping to eliminate the infection.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Hops have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with oral thrush.
Immune system support: Herbal hops creams may also have immunomodulatory effects, supporting the body's natural defenses against oral thrush and other infections.
remedy_parts: Barks: Rich in antifungal compounds that help combat the fungus causing oral thrush.
Roots: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm oral mucosa.
Leaves: Packed with antimicrobial agents that help eliminate fungal and bacterial infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 5-10 dried humulus lupulus flowers and 5-10 dried calendula officinalis flowers for their antifungal properties.
Combine 2 tablespoons of dried humulus lupulus flowers and 1 tablespoon of dried calendula officinalis flowers in a bowl.
Steep the mixture in 2 cups of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to obtain a liquid extract.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the herbal liquid extract with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax to create a creamy ointment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet briar creams helps with oral thrush because they contain antifungal properties that specifically target and eliminate the fungal infection. The herbs in these creams, such as rose petals and sweet briar root, have been used for centuries to treat various fungal infections, including oral thrush. By applying these creams directly to the affected area, they can provide quick relief from symptoms and help to prevent the spread of the infection.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Sweet briar creams can help reduce inflammation in the mouth, making it easier to heal and preventing further irritation.
Antifungal properties: Sweet briar creams contain compounds like salicylic acid that have antifungal properties, which can help combat the fungal infection that causes oral thrush.
Wound-healing properties: The tannins and other compounds in sweet briar creams can help stimulate the healing process of mouth sores and wounds, promoting the growth of new tissue and tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Antimicrobial properties help combat fungal infections.
Barks: Anti-inflammatory compounds reduce irritation and inflammation.
Seeds: Antifungal properties help control the spread of oral thrush.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh rosa rubiginosa flowers and dry them in a warm place for 7 days.
Grind 50g of dried rosa rubiginosa flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine 50g of the powder with 20g of beeswax, 10g of coconut oil, and 10g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture until the beeswax and shea butter are fully melted and the mixture is smooth for 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a clean jar and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry creams helps with oral thrush because of its antifungal properties that effectively target and eliminate the Candida fungus, the primary cause of the condition. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also reduce swelling and discomfort in the affected area, making it easier to manage symptoms. The highbush cranberry's natural acidity also helps maintain a healthy pH balance in the mouth, further inhibiting fungal growth and preventing the spread of oral thrush.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Highbush cranberry creams contain compounds that inhibit the growth of Candida, the fungus responsible for oral thrush, thereby reducing its severity.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, reducing pain and discomfort associated with oral thrush.

Antioxidant properties: Highbush cranberry's antioxidant properties help to combat oxidative stress and promote healing of damaged mucous membranes, supporting the body's natural defense against oral thrush.
remedy_recipe: Combine 1 cup of dried cranberries and 2 cups of distilled water in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over medium heat for 10-15 minutes or until the cranberries have broken down.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
Mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil into the cranberry mixture.
Store the cream in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antibacterial and antifungal compounds that help combat oral thrush infections.
Buds: Contain antimicrobial properties that help soothe and protect the affected area from further infection.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Loquat creams helps with oral thrush because of their antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The loquat extract, rich in flavonoids and carotenoids, inhibits the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. By soothing and calming the mucous membranes, loquat creams reduce inflammation and promote healing. Regular use can also help prevent recurrent thrush infections, providing relief and comfort to those suffering from this common condition. This makes loquat creams an effective and natural solution for oral thrush management.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Loquat extracts have shown antibacterial and antifungal properties, which could help combat the fungal overgrowth associated with oral thrush.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Loquat contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, which could help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the mouth associated with oral thrush.
Antioxidant activity: Loquat is rich in antioxidants, which could help protect oral tissues from oxidative stress and promote overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Barks: Contains compounds with anti-fungal and antibacterial properties that help to soothe and treat oral thrush.
Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help to combat fungal infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest eriobotrya japonica leaves and flowers in the early morning to ensure optimal potency and freshness.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area at room temperature for 7-10 days to preserve its properties.
Infuse 2 tablespoons of dried eriobotrya japonica in 1 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler for 2-3 hours.
Mix the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to create a smooth and creamy texture.
Pour the mixture into small glass containers and let it cool and harden at room temperature for 30 minutes to an hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with oral thrush because of its soothing and antimicrobial properties. The marshmallow root extract in these creams has been shown to reduce inflammation and kill Candida, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. The cream also helps to restore the mucous membranes' natural pH balance and promote healing, making it a gentle and effective treatment option for oral thrush. This can provide relief for individuals experiencing symptoms such as pain, discomfort, and difficulty swallowing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and soothing properties: Marshmallow creams help to reduce inflammation and soothe the mucous membranes in the mouth, providing relief from the discomfort and pain caused by oral thrush.

Mucilaginous properties: Marshmallow creams contain mucilage, a thick, gel-like substance that forms a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, helping to shield them from further irritation and promoting healing.

Antimicrobial and antifungal properties: Marshmallow creams contain compounds that have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal and bacterial pathogens, including Candida albicans, which is the primary cause of oral thrush.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Contains mucilages that soothe and protect oral mucous membranes.
Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit antifungal properties.
Buds: High in saponins that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100g of althaea officinalis root powder and add 500ml of distilled water to create a decoction.
Infuse the mixture for 2-3 hours or overnight to allow the plant's active compounds to release.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Mix 100g of beeswax, 100g of coconut oil, and 100g of shea butter with the strained decoction to create a smooth consistency.
Pour the mixture into containers and refrigerate for at least 2 hours to set and thicken before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for oral thrush are natural remedies that help alleviate symptoms and promote healing of the oral mucosa affected by the fungal infection. These creams typically contain a combination of herbs such as Melaleuca alternifolia, Echinacea purpurea, Aloe barbadensis, Calendula officinalis, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Zingiber officinale, Aloe vera, Eucalyptus globulus, Lavandula angustifolia, and Cinchona officinalis. If left untreated, oral thrush can cause discomfort, difficulty eating and speaking, and a weakened immune system, severely impacting one's daily life, affecting relationships, and hindering overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oral thrush that are treated with herbal creams are fungal infections such as vaginal yeast infections, diaper rash, and athlete's foot. These conditions are caused by overgrowth of fungi on the skin or mucous membranes, and herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, aloe vera, and neem oil can help to control the growth of fungi and soothe the affected area.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with oral thrush is a blend of tea tree oil, neem, and myrrh creams. Tea tree oil's antifungal properties combat the fungus causing thrush, while neem's antibacterial properties prevent secondary infections. Myrrh's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the affected areas, promoting faster healing. Applying these creams together in moderation can help alleviate oral thrush symptoms, such as redness, irritation, and white patches. This combination can be applied topically to the affected areas for optimal results.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with oral thrush because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound, tea tree oil, has been shown to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. By reducing the fungal load and inflammation, Melaleuca alternifolia creams promote a healthy oral environment, alleviating symptoms such as soreness, redness, and white patches. Its topical application makes it an effective and convenient treatment for oral thrush, supporting the body's natural healing process.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This monoterpenic compound has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush, and alleviate symptoms such as pain and discomfort.
Linalool: As a sesquiterpene, linalool exhibits antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in controlling the growth of Candida albicans and reducing inflammation in the mouth.
Cymene: This monoterpenic compound has antimicrobial properties, including antifungal activity, which can help to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans and alleviate symptoms of oral thrush.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of tea tree oil, which has antimicrobial properties that help combat fungal infections.
Stems: They also contain tea tree oil, which is extracted and used to treat oral thrush due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: The bark of Melaleuca alternifolia contains tea tree oil, which is used in creams to treat oral thrush due to its ability to reduce inflammation and prevent further infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves and flowers in morning after dew has fallen.
Dry the harvested plant material at 40°c for 2 days or until brittle using a dehydrator.
Grind 20g of dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 10g of the powdered plant material with 50g of coconut oil and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 10 minutes then pour into a tin and allow to cool completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with oral thrush because of its potent antimicrobial properties that target the fungus Candida albicans, a primary cause of the condition. The cream's active compounds, such as alkylamides and glycosides, work synergistically to inhibit fungal growth and reduce inflammation in the oral cavity. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea's immunomodulatory effects may help stimulate the body's natural defense mechanisms, further aiding in the treatment and prevention of oral thrush. This natural remedy offers a promising alternative to conventional treatments.
remedy_constituents: Icariin: It helps to prevent the growth and proliferation of fungal cells, including Candida albicans, which is the primary cause of oral thrush.
Cichoric acid: It has potent antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the severity of oral thrush symptoms and prevent further infections.
Caffeic acid: It exhibits antifungal activity by disrupting the cell membrane of fungal cells, ultimately leading to their death and preventing the spread of oral thrush.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea purpurea are commonly used due to their high content of polysaccharides and alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: The flowers of Echinacea purpurea are used because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antimicrobial properties that can help combat oral thrush.

Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea purpurea are often used due to their content of caffeic acid and rosmarinic acid, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can aid in healing and soothing oral thrush symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 4 oz of dried echinacea purpurea flowers in 2 cups of boiling distilled water for 10 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or a clean cotton cloth into a bowl.
Combine 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.
Add 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of the echinacea infusion to the melted beeswax mixture.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify for at least 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with oral thrush because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel of the Aloe vera plant contains compounds that have antimicrobial and antifungal effects, which can help to combat the fungal infection that causes oral thrush. The cream can also provide relief from the discomfort and pain associated with oral thrush, such as redness, swelling, and bleeding. Additionally, Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce swelling and promote healing in the affected area.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds have antimicrobial properties, which help combat the fungal infection causing oral thrush.
Saponins: Saponins exhibit anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, reducing inflammation and killing the fungal cells responsible for oral thrush.
Glycoproteins: Glycoproteins have been shown to stimulate the immune system, promoting the body's natural defense against fungal infections and helping to clear up oral thrush.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high content of aloe vera gel, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe and heal oral thrush.
Gel from the leaves: The gel extracted from the leaves is rich in vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that aid in reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of oral thrush lesions.

Aloe vera sap from the leaves: The sap from the leaves of Aloe barbadensis contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to have antifungal properties that can help combat the fungal infections causing oral thrush.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh aloe barbadensis gel from mature plants with thick green leaves.
Weigh and combine 20g of distilled water and 10g of glycerin in a small mixing bowl.
Mix 5g of candelilla wax and 5g of coconut oil in a separate heat-resistant container.
Combine the distilled water-glycerin mixture with the aloe barbadensis gel and heat the mixture until it reaches 75 celsius.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before whipping it into a smooth and creamy texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with oral thrush because of its potent antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and saponins, inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. Additionally, Calendula's soothing and protective qualities help to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with the condition. This natural remedy promotes a healthy oral environment, facilitating the recovery and prevention of oral thrush, making it a popular choice for those seeking a natural solution.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help to reduce inflammation and fight fungal infections by exhibiting antifungal and antimicrobial properties.
Triterpenoids: Exhibit antifungal and antibacterial activities that can help combat the Candida albicans fungus causing oral thrush.
Phenolic acids: Display antifungal and antioxidant properties, which can aid in reducing the severity of oral thrush symptoms and promoting healing in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their antifungal properties, which help combat the fungal infection causing oral thrush.
Leaves: They are used because they contain compounds that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, aiding in the treatment of oral thrush.
Seeds: They are used due to their ability to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried calendula flowers in the morning when dew is still present on the petals.
Infuse 2 cups of distilled water with 1 cup of dried flowers for 4 hours in a double boiler.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a liquid extract.
Combine 1/2 cup of the liquid extract with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a saucepan.
Stir the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes until it reaches a temperature of 120 degrees fahrenheit.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with oral thrush because of its anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The licorice root extract in these creams has been shown to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. The anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce redness and swelling in the affected areas, promoting a more comfortable healing environment. By tackling both the fungal infection and the inflammation, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams provide a comprehensive approach to treating oral thrush and promoting oral health.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These compounds exhibit antimicrobial properties, helping to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush.
Saponins: Saponins have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth affected by oral thrush.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of oral thrush symptoms, such as redness and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties.
Leaves: They are used due to their ability to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus that causes oral thrush.
Barks: They are used due to their antifungal properties, which help to combat the fungal infection that causes oral thrush.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 500ml of distilled water with 50g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root in a double boiler for 2 hours.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Combine 50g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil in a saucepan over low heat.
Stir the mixture of beeswax and coconut oil until it reaches a temperature of 60-70°c in 15 minutes.
Gradually add the infused liquid to the cooled mixture of beeswax and coconut oil while stirring until smooth and creamy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with oral thrush because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties. The active compound, gingerol, in Zingiber officinale has been shown to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. By reducing the fungal load, Zingiber officinale creams can help alleviate symptoms such as white patches, redness, and discomfort in the mouth and throat. This natural remedy promotes a healthy oral environment, allowing the body to fight off the infection and recover from oral thrush.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce inflammation and combat fungal infections in the oral cavity associated with oral thrush.
Shogaols: Shogaols have potent antimicrobial activity, particularly against fungal pathogens such as Candida, which cause oral thrush, thereby inhibiting the growth of the causative agent.
Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene has been shown to possess antifungal properties, aiding in the suppression of fungal growth and reducing the severity of oral thrush symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antifungal effects.
Barks: The barks contain bioactive compounds that have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which help in treating oral thrush.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes and wash them thoroughly in lukewarm water.
Dry the rhizomes in a warm place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind 50g of dried zingiber officinale into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Infuse 200ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler with 20g of zingiber officinale powder for 30 minutes.
Mix the infused oil with 50g of beeswax and 20ml of distilled water to create a smooth cream consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with oral thrush because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The gel extracted from the plant has been shown to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, reducing redness and discomfort associated with oral thrush. Additionally, aloe vera's antifungal properties can help combat the fungus Candida, which causes oral thrush. This natural remedy can also promote a healthy oral environment, allowing the body to fight off the infection and promoting faster healing and recovery.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin has antifungal properties, which help to inhibit the growth and proliferation of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush.
Saponins: Saponins have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which help to combat the infection and reduce inflammation in the mouth.

Vitamins and minerals (especially vitamin C and zinc): Vitamins and minerals in Aloe vera help to boost the immune system, reduce oxidative stress, and promote wound healing, all of which can aid in the recovery from oral thrush.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are widely used due to their high content of soothing compounds like aloe-emodin, which helps reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with oral thrush.
Gel from Leaves: The gel extracted from the leaves is also commonly used because it contains anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help combat fungal infections and promote healing.

Juice from Leaves: The juice from the leaves is another commonly used part of Aloe vera for making creams to treat oral thrush, as it has antimicrobial properties that help control the growth of fungal infections.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of pure aloe vera gel and mix it with 50g of vegetable oil in a blender.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the blender and blend until smooth.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160f for 10 minutes or until the wax and butter melt.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to 100f then whip it with an electric mixer.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with oral thrush because of its antifungal properties, which help combat the fungal infection that causes the condition. The eucalyptol present in the cream has been shown to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with oral thrush, promoting a healthy oral environment and alleviating symptoms. This makes Eucalyptus globulus creams a popular natural remedy for oral thrush.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol: Exhibits antimicrobial properties, helping to eliminate Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush.
Catechin: Possesses antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing fungal growth and inflammation associated with oral thrush.
Limonene: Demonstrates antifungal activity, contributing to the reduction of Candida albicans populations and alleviation of oral thrush symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make creams due to their high eucalyptol content, which has antimicrobial and antifungal properties to help combat oral thrush.
Barks: The barks of Eucalyptus globulus contain tannins and other compounds that have been traditionally used in oral care products to prevent and treat oral thrush.
Seeds: Some studies suggest that extracts from the seeds of Eucalyptus globulus may have antifungal properties, which could be beneficial in treating oral thrush when used in creams.
remedy_recipe: Melt 200g of shea butter in a double boiler over low heat for 10-15 minutes.
Combine 50g of beeswax with 100g of coconut oil in a saucepan over low heat.
Stir in 20g of dried eucalyptus leaves infused in 100ml of carrier oil for 30 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool for 10 minutes before whipping with an electric mixer.
Add 10 drops of vitamin e oil and 10 drops of tea tree oil to the mixture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with oral thrush because of its antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. Additionally, the cream's soothing and calming effects can help reduce discomfort, inflammation, and pain associated with the condition, promoting a healthy oral environment and facilitating the healing process. This natural remedy provides a gentle and non-invasive solution for oral thrush sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush.
Limonene: This terpene has antifungal properties, which can help inhibit the growth of Candida albicans and reduce the severity of oral thrush symptoms.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of oral thrush symptoms and promote healing in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Lavender flowers are commonly used due to their antifungal and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe and heal oral thrush.
Leaves: Lavender leaves contain compounds that exhibit antifungal and antibacterial properties, making them effective in treating oral thrush.
Seeds: Lavender seeds are also used, as they contain oils that have antiseptic and antifungal properties, which can aid in reducing the symptoms of oral thrush.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers and dry them for 2 weeks in a well-ventilated area.
Infuse 1 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at 70 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Combine 1 cup of infused oil with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax has melted completely.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool for 30 minutes before whipping it with a hand mixer until light and fluffy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams helps with oral thrush because it contains quinine, a natural antifungal compound that inhibits the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. The cream's quinine content reduces inflammation and soothes the affected area, promoting a healthy environment for the mouth. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis has antimicrobial properties, which help to eliminate the fungus and prevent its recurrence, making it a popular natural remedy for oral thrush. Regular application of the cream can alleviate symptoms and support oral health.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: This alkaloid has antifungal properties, helping to combat the fungal growth that causes oral thrush.
Quinovic acid glycosides: These compounds exhibit antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, aiding in the inhibition of fungal growth and reduction of symptoms associated with oral thrush.

Quinic acid: This compound is known for its antifungal and antibacterial properties, contributing to the relief of oral thrush symptoms by preventing fungal overgrowth and promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Alkaloids such as quinine and quinidine present in the leaves have antifungal properties that help combat oral thrush.
Barks: The barks contain cinchonine, an alkaloid that exhibits antifungal activity and helps alleviate symptoms of oral thrush.
Roots: The roots of Cinchona officinalis are a source of alkaloids like cinchonine and cinchonidine, which have been used to create creams for treating oral thrush.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 20 grams of dried cinchona officinalis bark in 1000 milliliters of carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 14 days.
Combine 200 milliliters of the infused oil with 100 grams of beeswax and 50 milliliters of distilled water.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a glass container and allow it to cool and thicken for 2 hours.
